message.content:
Репозиторий: /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP (монорепо: server/ Node+TS+Express+Mongoose+OpenAI; client/ React+Vite — это админка).
Ревью изменений ветки feat/knowledge-bank поверх базы 6431bf3..HEAD (2 коммита: админ-UI банка + переменный размер теста и качество вопросов).
Сначала прочитай диапазон диффа:  git -C /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP diff 6431bf3 HEAD -- <относящиеся пути>
Затем читай окружающий код, чтобы понять контекст. Ищи РЕАЛЬНЫЕ дефекты корректности и интеграции, а не стиль.
Контекст фич:
 - Бэкенд KC/банк (questionBank.service.ts, ktp.controller.ts, ktp.routes.ts): новый read-эндпоинт listItems вопросов банка (admin); size-параметр assembleNodeTest (разрешено 5/10/15/20), size подмешан в реюз-хэш; generateForCoverage переписан на циклы раундов + разброс difficulty; contentHashOf теперь хеширует текст+варианты+правильный ответ.
 - Test.model.ts: валидатор questions.length сменён с ===10 на диапазон 1..120.
 - test.routes.ts/test.controller.ts: /tests/node-bank принимает size; /generate(+guest) валидируют questionCount.
 - client: ktp.api.ts (+методы KC/банка), ktp.types.ts (+типы), components/ktp/TopicKnowledgeBank.tsx (новый), KtpCatalogPage.tsx (врезка per-topic), гейтинг по role==='admin'.
Возвращай только обоснованные находки с file/location/severity/detail/suggestedFix.

Адверсариально ПРОВЕРЬ находку — попробуй её ОПРОВЕРГНУТЬ, прочитав реальный код. Если это не настоящий дефект (ложноположительное, или уже корректно обрабатывается), верни isReal=false. По умолчанию при сомнении — isReal=false.

Находка:
{
  "file": "server/src/services/questionBank.service.ts",
  "location": "contentHashOf (строки 33-41)",
  "severity": "low",
  "title": "contentHashOf игнорирует correctOptions/correctMatching — для не-single_choice возможны коллизии дедупа",
  "detail": "Хэш строится только из questionText + options + correctOption. Модель QuestionItem допускает любой ENT-тип (questionType enum, correctOptions[], correctMatching), и тип IQuestion это поддерживает. Сейчас банк генерирует исключительно single_choice (generateKcQuestions жёстко задаёт single_choice и correctOption), поэтому на практике не стреляет. Но если в банк попадут multiple_choice/matching (через будущий updateItem из заложенного TODO или иной путь), два вопроса с одинаковым текстом/вариантами, но разными correctOptions/correctMatching дадут одинаковый contentHash и один из них будет молча отброшен по уникальному индексу как дубль.",
  "suggestedFix": "Подмешать в хэш correctOptions (отсортированные) и сериализованный correctMatching, либо явно зафиксировать инвариант 'банк хранит только single_choice' и валидировать questionType при вставке."
}

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll verify this finding by reading the actual code in question.
Let me read the surrounding code to understand the types and how questions flow into the bank.

input.command: grep -rn "interface IQuestion\b\|interface IQuestion " /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/ ; echo "---TYPES---"; grep -rn "IQuestion\b" /Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/types/ 2>/dev/null | head -30

content.text: Let me read the IQuestion interface and the generateKcQuestions function to confirm what types are actually produced.

content.text: Let me read the `generateKcQuestions` parser to confirm what fields it produces, and check the unique index definition on contentHash.

input.description: Inspect QuestionItem model for contentHash index and questionType validation

content.content:
/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts:34:    questionType: { type: String, enum: [...ENT_QUESTION_TYPES], default: 'single_choice' },
/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts:37:    correctOption: { type: String, trim: true },
/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts:38:    correctOptions: [{ type: String, trim: true }],
/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts:79:    contentHash: { type: String, required: true },
/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts:89:QuestionItemSchema.index({ subjectId: 1, knowledgeNodeId: 1, contentHash: 1 }, { unique: true });
/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts:90:QuestionItemSchema.index({ subjectId: 1, knowledgeNodeId: 1, status: 1 });
---FIND MODEL---
/Users/madiever/GenTestMVP/server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts

content.text:
Now I have a full picture. Let me verify the analysis:

**Confirmed facts:**
1. `contentHashOf` (lines 33-41) hashes only `questionText`, sorted `options`, and `correctOption` — it ignores `correctOptions` and `correctMatching`.
2. `IQuestion` (types/index.ts:166-187) supports `questionType` (enum), `correctOptions[]`, `correctMatching` — multiple ENT types.
3. The `QuestionItem` model (line 34) has `questionType` enum with `default: 'single_choice'` but **no validation restricting it to single_choice** — it accepts any `ENT_QUESTION_TYPES`.
4. The unique index (line 89) is `{ subjectId, knowledgeNodeId, contentHash }` — so a collision causes a silent dedup (code 11000 swallowed silently in the service).
5. `generateKcQuestions` (ai.service.ts:356-413) is hard-coded to single_choice via prompt, but uses `parseRegularQuestionsLenient` (a generic parser). It does **not** assert `questionType === 'single_choice'` post-parse. The prompt instructs single_choice, but there is no hard guarantee.

**Adversarial check — is this a REAL defect or false positive?**

The finding itself is explicitly self-aware: it states "Сейчас банк генерирует исключительно single_choice... поэтому на практике не стреляет." The severity is "low" and the scenario is **hypothetical/future** ("если в банк попадут multiple_choice/matching... через будущий updateItem из заложенного TODO"). 

Currently:
- The only write path is `generateForCoverage`, which calls `generateKcQuestions` (prompted for single_choice).
- The parser is lenient and generic, so in theory a misbehaving LLM could emit a non-single_choice question, but for it to cause an actual silent-dedup data loss, you'd need two questions with identical text + identical sorted options + identical (or absent) `correctOption`, differing only in `correctOptions`/`correctMatching`. For single_choice questions `correctOption` is always set, so the hash already differentiates them. The collision requires a non-single_choice type to be stored, which no current code path intentionally does.
- The `updateItem`/manual-edit path is an explicit TODO that is **not implemented**.

So this is not an active correctness/integration bug in the shipped diff. It is a latent/defensive concern about a future code path that does not yet exist, with no current trigger. Per the instructions (default to isReal=false when the defect does not currently fire and is already noted as non-firing), this is a false positive as a "real defect" in this branch. The hash change in this diff is in fact an *improvement* (text-only → text+options+correctOption); it merely doesn't extend coverage to question types that the bank never stores.

input.reason:
Проверил по реальному коду — это не активный дефект в данной ветке, а латентная защитная заметка о несуществующем пути.

Подтверждённые факты:
- contentHashOf (server/src/services/questionBank.service.ts:33-41) действительно хеширует только questionText + отсортированные options + correctOption, игнорируя correctOptions/correctMatching.
- IQuestion (server/src/types/index.ts:166-187) и модель QuestionItem (server/src/models/QuestionItem.model.ts:34, questionType enum с default 'single_choice', без restrict-валидатора) формально допускают любой ENT-тип.
- Уникальный индекс { subjectId, knowledgeNodeId, contentHash } (QuestionItem.model.ts:89); код 11000 молча проглатывается в generateForCoverage.

Почему НЕ реальный дефект:
1. Единственный путь записи в банк — generateForCoverage → aiService.generateKcQuestions, который промптом жёстко задаёт single_choice с обязательным correctOption (ai.service.ts:356-413, строки 379/385). Для single_choice correctOption всегда задан и уже входит в хэш, поэтому два по-разному ключёванных вопроса дадут разный хэш — коллизии нет.
2. Сценарий коллизии требует, чтобы в банк попал НЕ single_choice item (одинаковый текст+опции+correctOption, но разный correctOptions/correctMatching). Ни один текущий путь кода это намеренно не делает.
3. Путь, который мог бы это вызвать (updateItem/ручная правка), — явный нереализованный TODO (questionBank.service.ts, комментарий «Phase A-next ... не реализовано»).
4. Сама находка признаёт: «на практике не стреляет» и описывает гипотетическое будущее.

Изменение хэша в этом диффе фактически улучшение (text-only → text+options+correctOption). Расширение на correctOptions/correctMatching — разумная защита на будущее, но не дефект корректности/интеграции в текущей ветке. По правилу «при сомнении и при отсутствии текущего триггера → isReal=false» помечаю как ложноположительное.

content.text:
I verified the finding against the actual code and determined it is a false positive (isReal=false) for this branch.

The hash truly ignores `correctOptions`/`correctMatching`, and the model technically permits non-single_choice types. But there is no current code path that stores anything other than single_choice (the only writer, `generateForCoverage`, calls `generateKcQuestions`, which is prompt-locked to single_choice with a mandatory `correctOption`). For single_choice questions, `correctOption` is always set and already part of the hash, so no silent dedup collision can occur today. The collision scenario depends on a manual-edit/`updateItem` path that is an explicit unimplemented TODO. The finding itself concedes it "doesn't fire in practice." Per the default-to-false guidance when a defect has no active trigger, this is a latent/defensive concern rather than a real correctness or integration bug in this diff.
